touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Samuel O'Connor at KineticLabs. No answer, left voicemail introducing our forecasting automation platform.
Second cold call attempt to Samuel O'Connor. No answer, no voicemail left this time.
Cold email sent to Samuel O'Connor highlighting how similar eCommerce companies improved forecast accuracy by 40%. No response received.

Connected with Samuel O'Connor for 8-minute intro call. He expressed interest in improving their sales forecasting process, which is currently manual and error-prone. Scheduled discovery call for next week.

questions_asked.question: What are your current challenges with sales forecasting?
questions_asked.answer: We're using spreadsheets and gut feel. Our forecast variance is around 30%, which makes resource planning difficult.

questions_asked.question: How quickly can your platform be implemented?
questions_asked.answer: Typically 2-3 weeks for initial setup, with full rollout in 4-6 weeks depending on data integrations.

next_steps: Schedule discovery call for next Tuesday
Send calendar invite and prep materials

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with meeting confirmation and brief case study on eCommerce forecasting. No response needed.
Qualification email sent asking about team size, current tools, and decision timeline. Samuel responded confirming 27-person sales team and interest in Q2 implementation.

questions_asked.question: How many people are on your sales team currently?
questions_asked.answer: We have 27 account executives across our North America and EMEA regions.

next_steps: Prepare discovery call agenda

touches.ai_summary: Pre-discovery email sent with agenda and questions about their tech stack. Samuel confirmed they use Salesforce and are open to API integrations.

questions_asked.question: What CRM platform are you currently using?
questions_asked.answer: We're on Salesforce Sales Cloud, been using it for about 3 years.

touches.ai_summary: 19-minute follow-up call to dive deeper into their forecasting pain points. Samuel described manual weekly forecast reviews taking 4-5 hours and frequent pipeline surprises. He mentioned budget is available but needs to build internal case.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through your current forecasting process each week.
questions_asked.answer: Every Friday our AEs update Salesforce, then I spend 4-5 hours reviewing, adjusting, and creating reports for the executive team. It's painful.

questions_asked.question: What kind of accuracy improvement do your customers typically see?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers reduce forecast variance from 25-30% down to under 10% within the first quarter.

questions_asked.question: Who else would be involved in evaluating a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: Primarily me and my sales ops manager. Our CFO cares about the output but won't be in the weeds on vendor selection.

objections: Need to understand ROI clearly before bringing this to leadership
next_steps: Schedule full discovery call with sales ops manager included
Send ROI calculator template

touches.ai_summary: 17-minute discovery call focused on technical requirements and use cases. Samuel explained their seasonal demand patterns and need for automation. Discussed integration with Salesforce and data requirements.

questions_asked.question: What specific metrics do you need to forecast beyond revenue?
questions_asked.answer: Revenue is primary, but we also need deal count and average deal size by segment. Seasonal patterns are important for us.

questions_asked.question: How much historical data do you need to make this work?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally 12-18 months, but we can work with as little as 6 months of clean pipeline data.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like for you in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: If we could get forecast variance under 15% and cut my weekly review time in half, that would be a huge win.

next_steps: Schedule product demo for next week
Send technical integration overview

touches.ai_summary: 52-minute product demo with Samuel and sales ops manager. Walked through forecasting dashboard, automation capabilities, and Salesforce integration. Samuel was engaged and asked detailed questions about customization and reporting. Team seemed impressed with accuracy metrics and automation potential.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the forecast models based on our specific business cycles?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, the platform learns from your historical patterns and allows manual adjustments for known seasonal events or business changes.

questions_asked.question: How does this handle our EMEA region which has different seasonality?
questions_asked.answer: You can create separate models by region, segment, or product line, each with its own parameters.

questions_asked.question: What kind of timeline are you working with for implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally we'd start in May, get it dialed in by end of Q2 before our busy season in Q3.

questions_asked.question: What level of ongoing support comes with the platform?
questions_asked.answer: All plans include email support, and the unlimited plan includes a dedicated success manager and quarterly business reviews.

objections: Want to make sure we have enough time for proper implementation before our busy season
next_steps: Send pricing proposal with unlimited automation tier
Schedule proposal review call
Provide implementation timeline overview

touches.ai_summary: 13-minute proposal review call. Presented unlimited automation tier at $20,200 ACV for 27 seats. Samuel expressed concern about budget and asked about discounting options. Discussed payment terms and contract length.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the pricing? This is above what I had budgeted.
questions_asked.answer: Let me see what we can do. If you can commit to an annual contract and close by end of month, I can work with my manager on a discount.

questions_asked.question: What budget range were you planning for?
questions_asked.answer: I was thinking closer to $15K for the year. The ROI is there, but I need to stay within my allocated budget.

objections: Pricing is higher than budgeted amount
next_steps: Get approval for discounted pricing
Send revised proposal by end of week
Schedule follow-up to review revised terms

touches.ai_summary: Email sent with revised proposal reflecting 34% discount, bringing ACV to $13,300. Samuel responded positively, saying this works within budget and he'll review with his sales ops manager.
next_steps: Wait for internal review
Schedule negotiation call for next week

touches.ai_summary: 26-minute negotiation call to finalize terms. Samuel confirmed the discounted pricing works and he's ready to move forward. Discussed contract start date, payment terms, and implementation kickoff. He mentioned he'll handle internal approvals.

questions_asked.question: Can we start the contract on May 1st to align with our fiscal quarter?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can set May 1st as the start date and backdate the contract if needed.

questions_asked.question: Do you need anyone else to review the contract before signing?
questions_asked.answer: Just our procurement team for standard vendor review. Shouldn't be an issue.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we want to add more seats mid-contract?
questions_asked.answer: You can add seats anytime, prorated for the remainder of the contract term.

next_steps: Send final contract for legal review
Schedule exec alignment meeting to confirm implementation timeline
Get procurement contacts for vendor setup

touches.ai_summary: 43-minute executive alignment meeting with Samuel and sales ops manager to confirm implementation plan and success metrics. Walked through onboarding timeline, data migration, training schedule, and Q3 readiness goals. Team confirmed commitment and signed off on project plan.

questions_asked.question: Who will be the main point of contact during implementation?
questions_asked.answer: I'll be primary contact, with my sales ops manager handling the technical coordination.

questions_asked.question: When can we start the Salesforce data audit?
questions_asked.answer: Our implementation team can begin the data audit within 48 hours of contract signature.

questions_asked.question: What if we're not seeing the accuracy improvements by end of Q2?
questions_asked.answer: We have a 90-day success guarantee. If we're not hitting targets, we'll adjust the models and extend support until we get there.

questions_asked.question: Should we plan to include the CFO in any of the training sessions?
questions_asked.answer: We can do an executive overview session to show them the dashboards they'll see, but they won't need hands-on training.

next_steps: Contract to procurement for final signature
Schedule implementation kickoff for first week of May
Send welcome packet and training materials
